content.content:
975	
976	
977	/*
978	** Stripped-down 'require': After checking "loaded" table, calls 'openf'
979	** to open a module, registers the result in 'package.loaded' table and,
980	** if 'glb' is true, also registers the result in the global table.
981	** Leaves resulting module on the top.
982	*/
983	LUALIB_API void luaL_requiref (lua_State *L, const char *modname,
984	                               lua_CFunction openf, int glb) {
985	  luaL_getsubtable(L, LUA_REGISTRYINDEX, LUA_LOADED_TABLE);
986	  lua_getfield(L, -1, modname);  /* LOADED[modname] */
987	  if (!lua_toboolean(L, -1)) {  /* package not already loaded? */
988	    lua_pop(L, 1);  /* remove field */
989	    lua_pushcfunction(L, openf);
990	    lua_pushstring(L, modname);  /* argument to open function */
991	    lua_call(L, 1, 1);  /* call 'openf' to open module */
992	    lua_pushvalue(L, -1);  /* make copy of module (call result) */
993	    lua_setfield(L, -3, modname);  /* LOADED[modname] = module */
994	  }
995	  lua_remove(L, -2);  /* remove LOADED table */
996	  if (glb) {
997	    lua_pushvalue(L, -1);  /* copy of module */
998	    lua_setglobal(L, modname);  /* _G[modname] = module */
999	  }
1000	}
1001	
1002	
1003	LUALIB_API void luaL_addgsub (luaL_Buffer *b, const char *s,
1004	                                     const char *p, const char *r) {

content.text:
Build is green.

## Report

**Modified:** `crates/lua-stdlib/src/state_stub.rs` — overrode the `require_lib` method in the `impl LuaStateStubExt for LuaState` block (lines 291–295). Reverted the trait's default body, since default-method bodies see `self: &mut Self` (not `&mut LuaState`), which fails to coerce into `&mut LuaState` for the helper call.

**Behavior:** `require_lib(name, openf, glb)` now delegates to the existing Phase-A free function `crate::auxlib::requiref(self, name, openf, glb)`, which faithfully ports `luaL_requiref` from `lauxlib.c:983`: fetch `LOADED` subtable from the registry, look up `LOADED[name]`; if falsy, call `openf(name)` and store the result; remove the LOADED table; if `glb`, also assign to `_G[name]`.

**Next todo!() likely to surface (called transitively via `auxlib::requiref`):** `get_subtable` → `state.get_subtable_registry`, `get_field`, `to_boolean`, `pop_n`, `push_c_function`, `push_bytes`, `call`, `push_value`, `set_field`, `remove`, `set_global`. The implement loop will catch the next one.

**`cargo build -p lua-cli`:** green (warnings only).
